metadata.title: How do small councils compare to the state?
metadata.subtitle: Small councils are often assumed to sit behind the broader sector. The real picture is more uneven - and more useful.
metadata.prototypeLabel: Workspace data snapshot - mixed governance status
metadata.caveat: This version uses real local EBA Workbench datamart values, including governed pay-derived rows and staged or partial downstream assets. It is suitable for product demonstration and analytical review, not external decision-making without governance sign-off.
metadata.futureSourcePath: Replace this file with a reviewed report_product_input_mart payload once the report asset is promoted.

snapshotRule.payRows: Report date must fall inside the pay row operative window.
snapshotRule.operativeTo: pay_range_summary_mart.effective_to, or effective_from + 1 calendar year when effective_to is blank
snapshotRule.councilEligibility: canonical council must be active in council_profile_mart

reportManifest.title: Small Council vs State Scrollytelling Prototype - Workspace Snapshot
reportManifest.report_title_candidate: How do small councils compare to the state?
reportManifest.metric_definition: Active weekly range midpoint rates by standard band, comparing council_category=Small shire with active statewide rows.

heroMetrics.label: Band 5 median gap
heroMetrics.detail: Small shire below statewide weekly midpoint

heroMetrics.label: Agreement timing coverage
heroMetrics.detail: Small shire Band 5 rows in horizon analysis

payPointGalaxy.question: What does the whole pay field look like over time?
payPointGalaxy.title: Pay point galaxy
payPointGalaxy.summary: Each point is an average weekly pay value for a cohort, standard band, level label, and effective date. The view turns the pay mart from a table into a temporal field of observations.
payPointGalaxy.metric: Average weekly rate, using step_mean_weekly_rate where available and range_midpoint_weekly_rate as fallback

cohorts.label: Small shire
cohorts.description: Rows where council_profile_mart.council_category equals Small shire.

summaryMetrics.label: Pay summary rows
summaryMetrics.detail: statewide rows with usable weekly values

summaryMetrics.label: Galaxy points
summaryMetrics.detail: cohort x date x band x level averages

payPointGalaxy.caveat: This is a real local workspace aggregation, not a final governed product view. It compares available mart rows over all effective dates and does not apply headcount weighting, agreement recency filtering, or final coverage governance.

cohort.title: Small shire is a controlled cohort, not a handpicked peer set.
cohort.definition: This version uses the controlled council_category field from council_profile_mart: Small shire.
cohort.smallCouncilDefinition: Victorian councils where council_category equals Small shire in the local council profile mart.
cohort.statewideDefinition: All councils with active standard band range midpoint rows as at 2026-01-01.
cohort.productionCaveat: The cohort definition is real local metadata, but final production reporting should still carry governance status and coverage warnings.

map.title: Victoria LGA boundary context

legend.label: Small shire with active pay rows

map.caveat: Boundary shading uses controlled council profile metadata. Active pay coverage is narrower than full council coverage and should be carried as a report warning.

evidenceChain.description: Small shire membership comes from controlled council profile metadata.
Active weekly midpoint rows are derived from pay_range_summary_mart.
Distribution framing follows the existing distribution-point report asset contract.
Scheduled horizon changes use uplift_timing_mart where rules land before the horizon date.
This standalone web report remains a draft product layer until reviewed.

state_only.label: Statewide active median
state_only.summary: Start with active statewide midpoint rows for Bands 1-8.

small_vs_state.label: Small shire vs state
small_vs_state.summary: Layer the controlled Small shire cohort over the active statewide pattern.

gap_highlight.label: Largest observed gaps
gap_highlight.summary: The largest weekly median gap in this snapshot is Band 7 (-65.29).

band_focus.label: Narrower gaps
band_focus.summary: Some bands sit closer, which changes the executive interpretation.

timing_sensitive.label: Timing sensitive
timing_sensitive.summary: Bands 5 and 6 are the pressure point and connect directly to the uplift horizon.

takeaway.label: Takeaway
takeaway.summary: The live workspace data shows a consistent Small shire discount, but the size and meaning vary by band.

payByBand.takeaway: In the active 2026-01-01 workspace snapshot, Small shire medians sit below the statewide median across Bands 1-8. The gap is most commercially interesting around Bands 5-6: Band 5 is $44/week lower and Band 6 is $57/week lower on the explicit range_midpoint_rate metric.

distribution.question: Is the gap uniform, or does it depend where you look?
distribution.summary: The active Band 5 distribution is not a smooth bell curve. It shows at least two concentrations of councils, and the Small shire median sits below the statewide median while still overlapping other cohort ranges.
distribution.sourceDataset: pay_range_summary_mart active snapshot, cross-checked against the distribution prototype contract
distribution.inclusionRule: Includes councils with an eligible Band 5 range_midpoint_weekly_rate row where the report date 2026-01-01 falls between effective_from and effective_to. If effective_to is blank, the operative window is effective_from through effective_from + 1 calendar year. canonical_council_id must also resolve to council_profile_mart with status=active and is_active=True.

shapeDiagnostics.reading: 2 material peaks in the raw midpoint distribution.
shapeDiagnostics.method: Dynamic local-maximum scan over a fixed 14-bin histogram using raw midpoint values; bins with fewer than two councils or below 25% of the largest bin are not labelled as primary peaks.

primaryPeaks.description: Most common cohort in this band: Small shire.

primaryPeaks.description: Most common cohort in this band: Metropolitan.

shapeDiagnostics.interpretation: Peak labels are generated from the active Band 5 values, so the annotation follows the current snapshot instead of fixed narrative text.
shapeDiagnostics.caveat: Peak detection is bin-sensitive and should be treated as a descriptive diagnostic, not a formal distribution test.

distribution.caveat: Workspace snapshot from active pay range summaries. This uses real local workbench values but still needs final report governance before publication.

classificationContext.question: Are we comparing the same workforce shape?
classificationContext.summary: Band-level comparison reduces noise, but it does not remove workforce-shape risk. The active pay rows show stronger mid-band exposure, where councils often face professional, technical, supervisory, and specialist recruitment pressure.
classificationContext.productionNote: Production reporting should add workforce composition weights; this prototype compares available active band midpoint rows without headcount weighting.

upliftTimeline.question: Is this a structural pay gap, or a timing artefact?
upliftTimeline.summary: Snapshot comparisons can mislead when councils sit at different points in their agreement cycle.

phases.label: Current snapshot
phases.description: Compare active Band 5 midpoint rates as at the report date.

phases.label: Scheduled uplifts
phases.description: Reveal governed uplift rules that land before the horizon date.

phases.label: Horizon view
phases.description: Project the same rows after known scheduled uplift events.

series.name: Small shire median
series.cycleStatus: 9 of 15 Band 5 rows have scheduled uplifts to 2027-07-01

series.name: Statewide median
series.cycleStatus: 37 of 52 Band 5 rows have scheduled uplifts to 2027-07-01

uplifts.label: 3% or 90% of Rate Cap, whichever is higher
3% or 90% of Rate Cap, whichever is higher

upliftTimeline.caveat: Real uplift rules from uplift_timing_mart are applied mechanically to active Band 5 midpoint rows. Dollar floors are approximated as weekly increments where the rule states a weekly basis.

entitlements.question: Do small councils compete differently?
entitlements.summary: The local entitlement mart is currently a staged taxonomy, not a governed council-by-council comparison. It is still useful here because it shows the employment-value categories the reporting layer can connect to next.

Pay.label: Below median
Pay.note: Real Band 5-6 active snapshot sits below statewide median.

Leave.label: 15 taxonomy items
Leave.note: Staged entitlement taxonomy, not presence scoring.

Flexibility.label: 4 condition items
Flexibility.note: Potential employment-value lever, not yet scored.

Progression.label: Pay ladder context
Progression.note: Progression is visible in pay range summaries; rule-level progression remains partial.

Pay.label: Benchmark
Pay.note: All active councils in the local snapshot.

entitlements.caveat: Entitlement content is real local taxonomy from entitlement_summary_mart, but it is staged_not_governed and must not be read as council-level entitlement evidence.

executiveTakeaways.title: Exposure
executiveTakeaways.body: Small shire medians are below the active statewide median across all analysed bands, with the most useful sales/demo story around Bands 5-6.

executiveTakeaways.title: Distribution
executiveTakeaways.body: The Band 5 distribution shows overlap with the state, so the story is not a simplistic lower-versus-higher binary.

executiveTakeaways.title: Timing risk
executiveTakeaways.body: Known future uplift rules narrow or reshape some gaps by the horizon date, which is exactly where the workbench adds value.

narrativeSteps.title: Start with the active state
narrativeSteps.body: The statewide line now uses real active range midpoint rows from the local mart, not placeholder values.

narrativeSteps.title: Layer the Small shire cohort
narrativeSteps.body: The controlled Small shire cohort sits below the state across each standard band in this workspace snapshot.

narrativeSteps.title: The gap concentrates in mid and upper bands
narrativeSteps.body: Bands 5 and 6 are the sharper executive story because the weekly difference is larger and easier to connect to attraction and retention pressure.

narrativeSteps.title: Lower bands are closer
narrativeSteps.body: The lower-band gap exists, but the magnitude is smaller. That matters when deciding where exposure is operationally meaningful.

narrativeSteps.title: The pay horizon changes the reading
narrativeSteps.body: The current snapshot is not the full answer. Scheduled uplifts can narrow, widen, or re-order the comparison by the next horizon.

narrativeSteps.title: The answer is real, but conditional
narrativeSteps.body: The workspace data supports a sharper claim than the placeholder prototype: Small shire councils sit lower on active midpoint pay, but the report needs distribution, timing, and coverage caveats to be credible.
